Quantum Dice and NATS (UK Air Traffic Control) are pleased to announce a collaboration to advance the application of probabilistic methods in Air Traffic Management (ATM).
Together, the organisations aim to pioneer new modelling approaches for next-generation ATM environments.
Quantum Dice and NATS (UK Air Traffic Control) are pleased to announce a collaboration to advance the application of probabilistic methods in Air Traffic Management (ATM).
Together, the organisations aim to pioneer new modelling approaches for next-generation ATM environments.

At Quantum Dice, we believe probabilistic approaches will be key to modelling and managing complex situations, and we're excited to collaborate with NATS to explore how they can support more resilient airspace operations.

Through this collaboration, we will investigate the ways in which these advanced methods might enhance the resilience and predictive power of our systems, ensuring we stay ahead of the increasing complexity of air traffic in the UK.

Quantum Dice is a University of Oxford spin-out bringing to market the first scalable probabilistic computing platform to help tackle some of the world's most critical problems: efficiently enabling the next generation of energy-based AI models and solving the complex optimisation problems increasingly encountered in modern industry.

interaction_phrase: secured a €2 million grant from the European Innovation Council
occurrence_sentence: Quantum Dice, a UK-based leader in quantum photonics and probabilistic computing, has secured a €2 million grant from the European Innovation Council (EIC) Accelerator programme, with the project now officially underway following its October kick-off.

interaction_phrase: secured a €2 million grant from the European Innovation Council (EIC) Accelerator programme
occurrence_sentence: Quantum Dice, a UK-based leader in quantum photonics and probabilistic computing, has secured a €2 million grant from the European Innovation Council (EIC) Accelerator programme, with the project now officially underway following its October kick-off.

interaction_phrase: CEO and Co-founder of Quantum Dice
occurrence_sentence: "Receiving support from the EIC Accelerator is a strong validation of our progress and the transformative commercial potential of our probabilistic computing platform. We are excited to see that the EIC shares our vision concerning the importance of developing the next generation of computing hardware to tackle the ever more complex problems we are facing." — Dr Ramy Shelbaya, CEO and Co-founder of Quantum Dice
